name: Blueberry/Sour Cream Coffeecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the butter and 1/2 cup of sugar until blended.
2. Gradually add the remaining sugar, beating until light and fluffy (about 2 minutes).
3. Add eggs, sour cream, and vanilla extract, mixing for an additional 2 minutes.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together.
5. Stir the dry ingredients into the butter mixture just enough to combine.
6. Spoon half of the batter into a greased 9 or 10 inch tube pan or 9 x 13 pan, spreading evenly.
7. In another mixing bowl, combine brown sugar, cinnamon, remaining flour, and remaining butter until crumbly.
8. Sprinkle half of this topping over the batter in the pan.
9. Evenly sprinkle blueberries over the topping.
10. Drop spoonfuls of remaining batter over the blueberries and spread evenly with a rubber spatula.
11. Sprinkle the remaining cinnamon topping over the cake.
12. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
13. Remove from oven and let cool in the pan on a wire rack for 5 minutes.
14. Place a cookie sheet over the top of the coffee cake, invert the cake onto the cookie sheet, and remove from the pan.
15. Turn the cake right side up again and place it on a wire rack to complete cooling.
Note: This recipe freezes well.
